title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for a delicious lunch or dinner.
longTailKeywords: how to make Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani
Butter Chicken recipe step by step
seo.h1: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
How to Make Butter Chicken: Step-by-Step Guide
Tips for the Perfect Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of homemade Butter Chicken, a classic Indian Murgh Makhani dish perfect for any meal.

description: Savor the rich and creamy flavors of authentic Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian dish known as Murgh Makhani. Tender chicken pieces are simmered in a luscious tomato-based gravy, enriched with butter, cream, and aromatic spices. This comforting and indulgent curry is perfect served with naan or rice, offering a taste of Indias culinary heritage.

ingredients.notes: from 1/4 cup cashews soaked in hot water and blended

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well, cover, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 4 hours.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken and cook in batches until lightly browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Remove chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Add an additional teaspoon of ginger-garlic paste if desired.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens slightly. Add the cashew paste, honey or sugar, and kasoori methi. Mix well and bring to a gentle simmer.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the cooked chicken to the pan with the tomato gravy. Stir gently to coat the chicken. Pour in the heavy cream and mix. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes over low heat, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken to your desired consistency.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with naan bread, roti, or basmati rice for a complete Indian meal.

tips: For extra smoky flavor, you can grill or pan-fry the marinated chicken before adding it to the sauce.
Soaking cashews in hot water for 15-20 minutes before blending creates a smoother, richer paste for the gravy.
Always add cream at the very end and simmer gently to prevent it from splitting.
article:
Butter Chicken, known in Hindi as Murgh Makhani, is more than just a dish; it's a culinary hug in a bowl. This iconic Indian lunch recipe has captivated palates worldwide with its luscious, creamy texture, rich tomato-based gravy, and tender pieces of marinated chicken. Perfect for a comforting mid-day meal, Butter Chicken offers a delightful balance of tanginess, subtle sweetness, and mild spice, making it a universally loved choice for both seasoned Indian food enthusiasts and newcomers alike.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

Murgh Makhani, or Butter Chicken, literally translates to "chicken with butter," and this name perfectly encapsulates the essence of this beloved dish. Originating in Delhi, India, in the 1950s, it's believed to have been created by Kundan Lal Jaggi and Kundan Lal Gujral at Moti Mahal restaurant. The story goes that they combined leftover tandoori chicken with a rich tomato and butter-based gravy, and a legend was born.

Unlike some spicier Indian curries, Murgh Makhani is renowned for its mild, approachable flavor profile. The chicken is typically marinated in yogurt and a blend of spices before being cooked, often in a tandoor (clay oven), though pan-frying or grilling also works well. The magic, however, truly happens in the sauce: a velvety smooth concoction of puréed tomatoes, ginger, garlic, cashews, and a generous amount of butter and cream. It's the harmonious blend of these ingredients that gives Butter Chicken its signature taste and luxurious texture, making it a staple in Indian cuisine and a frequent choice for an indulgent Indian lunch.

## Ingredients for Butter Chicken

Crafting authentic Butter Chicken requires a few key components. Here's a breakdown of the essential ingredients you'll need for both the chicken and the rich makhani sauce:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into bite-sized pieces
*   Plain yogurt
*   Ginger-garlic paste
*   Lemon juice
*   Red chili powder (Kashmiri for color, or regular for heat)
*   Turmeric powder
*   Garam masala
*   Salt

For the Makhani Sauce:
*   Unsalted butter
*   Oil (vegetable or canola)
*   Onions, finely chopped or puréed
*   Ginger-garlic paste
*   Canned crushed tomatoes or fresh ripe tomatoes, puréed
*   Cashews (soaked in warm water for 15-20 minutes)
*   Green chilies (optional, for a touch of heat)
*   Spices: Cumin powder, coriander powder, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala
*   Fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), crushed
*   Heavy cream
*   Sugar (to balance acidity)
*   Salt to taste
*   Water or chicken broth (to adjust consistency)

## How to Make Butter Chicken: Step-by-Step Guide

Making homemade Butter Chicken is a rewarding experience. Follow these steps for a truly delicious result:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken**: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring each piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours, for maximum flavor.
2.  **Cook the Chicken**: Heat a little oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until lightly charred and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Set aside. If you have a grill or air fryer, you can achieve a similar smoky flavor.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base**: In the same pan (or a clean one), melt butter and a little oil over medium heat. Add chopped onions and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
4.  **Add Tomatoes and Spices**: Pour in the puréed tomatoes. Add the powdered spices (cumin, coriander, red chili, turmeric, garam masala, and salt). Cook, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes, allowing the tomatoes to break down and the oil to separate from the mixture.
5.  **Incorporate Cashews**: Add the soaked and drained cashews to the tomato mixture. Stir well and cook for another 5 minutes.
6.  **Blend the Sauce**: Carefully transfer the entire mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky sauce, you can pass it through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.
7.  **Finish the Sauce**: Return the smooth sauce to the pan over low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces. Stir in the crushed kasoori methi, sugar, and adjust salt if needed. Simmer for 5-10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
8.  **Add Cream and Butter**: Finally, stir in the heavy cream and a generous knob of butter. Heat through gently, but do not boil after adding cream, as it can curdle.
9.  **Garnish and Serve**: Garnish with fresh cilantro and a swirl of cream, if desired. Your homemade Butter Chicken is ready!

## Tips for the Perfect Butter Chicken

Achieving that restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is easier with these expert tips:

*   **Marination Time is Key**: The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight marination truly makes a difference.
*   **Use Quality Tomatoes**: Fresh, ripe tomatoes will yield the best flavor, but high-quality canned crushed tomatoes are a great substitute, offering consistency.
*   **Don't Skip the Cashews**: Cashews are crucial for the sauce's characteristic creaminess and body. Soak them beforehand to ensure they blend smoothly.
*   **Blend Thoroughly**: A truly smooth sauce is the hallmark of Murgh Makhani. Blend until there are no discernible chunks, and consider straining for ultimate silkiness.
*   **Kasoori Methi is Essential**: Dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) impart a unique, slightly bitter, yet aromatic depth to the sauce that is indispensable for authentic flavor. Gently rub them between your palms before adding to release their aroma.
*   **Balance Flavors**: Taste and adjust. A good Butter Chicken has a delicate balance of sweet (from sugar/honey), tangy (from tomatoes), and richness (from butter/cream).
*   **Smoke (Dhungar Method)**: For an authentic tandoori flavor, you can try the "dhungar" method: place a small, burning charcoal in a small heatproof bowl inside the pot of finished curry. Drizzle a little ghee over the charcoal, cover immediately, and let it infuse for 5-10 minutes.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments, making it a perfect center-piece for any Indian lunch.

*   **Naan Bread**: The classic pairing! Warm, fluffy naan is perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich sauce. Garlic naan or butter naan elevate the experience further.
*   **Basmati Rice**: Steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a neutral base that beautifully complements the curry's robust flavors.
*   **Roti or Paratha**: For a lighter option, whole wheat roti or flaky parathas are excellent choices.
*   **Fresh Salad**: A simple side salad with cucumber, tomato, and onion can offer a refreshing contrast to the richness of the curry.
*   **Raita**: A cooling yogurt dip, such as cucumber raita, can provide a nice balance, especially if you've added a touch more spice to your Butter Chicken.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the traditional Butter Chicken recipe is divine, there are many ways to adapt it to dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani**: Easily substitute chicken with paneer (Indian cheese) for Paneer Makhani, or use tofu, chickpeas, or mixed vegetables for a delicious vegetarian alternative.
*   **Healthier Options**: Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or substitute some of the cream with milk or even a plant-based cream alternative. You can also grill or bake the chicken instead of pan-frying.
*   **Spice Level**: For a spicier version, increase the amount of red chili powder or add finely chopped green chilies to the sauce base. For milder palates, use Kashmiri red chili powder which provides color without much heat.
*   **Nut-Free**: If you have a nut allergy, you can substitute cashews with sunflower seeds, or use a tablespoon of gram flour (besan) roasted in a little oil to thicken the sauce, though the flavor profile will be slightly different.
*   **Vegan Version**: Use plant-based chicken substitutes, cook in oil instead of butter, and use a cashew-heavy cream or coconut cream for the sauce.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani remains a timeless classic, a testament to the comforting and rich flavors of Indian cuisine. Whether you're making it for a special occasion or a simple, satisfying Indian lunch, this recipe promises a meal that's both flavorful and fulfilling. Enjoy the process of creating this culinary masterpiece in your own kitchen!
